creator: lennox@cs.columbia.edu
text:
This is a new port of IBM's International Components for Unicode
(ICU).

This is my first FreeBSD port, so I'd appreciate it if someone more
experienced could look it over to make sure I've done everything correctly.
In particular, I'm not sure I handled the installation of the documentation
correctly, and I'm dubious about my use of the INSTALLS_SHLIB variable.
(Without this variable 'make deinstall' did not correctly de-install the
shared libraries, however.)

This port was derived from a NetBSD package for an earlier version of ICU.
I've modified it substatially, however.

pkg-descr follows:

The International Components for Unicode (ICU) are C and C++ libraries
that provide robust and full-featured Unicode support on a wide
variety of platforms. The libraries provide:

Calendar support
Character set conversions
Collation (language-sensitive)
Date & time formatting
Locales (170+ supported)
Resource bundles
Message formatting 
Normalization 
Number & currency formatting
Time zones
Transliteration
Word, line & sentence breaks

WWW: http://oss.software.ibm.com/icu/

Port derived from NetBSD package by skrll@netbsd.org.

Jonathan Lennox
lennox@cs.columbia.edu
